title: koeleria macrantha var. rigidula
intro: Koeleria macrantha var. rigidula, commonly known as prairie junegrass, is a type of grass native to North America, characterized by its dense, clump-forming growth habit and feathery plumes. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. Consuming prairie junegrass has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. Prairie junegrass can be prepared for herbal use through infusion, decoction, or tincture, with the dried plant material typically used as a tea or added to food products for its flavor and nutritional value.
